heading: Hospital vessels in time of war
label: 60311 Hospital vessels in time of war
text: In time of war, a hospital vessel is exempt from tonnage taxes, light money, and pilotage charges in the ports of the United States if the vessel is one for which the conditions of the international convention for the exemption of hospital ships from taxation in time of war, concluded at The Hague on , are satisfied. The President by proclamation shall name the vessels for which the conditions are satisfied and state when the exemption begins and ends.December 21, 1904
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)6031146 App.:133., .Mar. 24, 1908, ch. 9635 Stat. 4646 App.:134.The words “and possessions thereof” are omitted as unnecessary because of the definition of “United States” in chapter 1 of the revised title.
